title: Listen and Choose a Response — Technology & Communication

questions.stem: The campus Wi-Fi keeps disconnecting in my dorm. Is there a solution?

options.A: Try registering your device through the IT portal. That usually stabilizes the connection.
options.C: I use mobile data on my phone.

questions.explanation: The speaker is reporting a technical issue, so providing a specific troubleshooting step is the most helpful response.

questions.stem: Does the school provide free access to Microsoft Office for students?

options.A: Yes, all students can download Office 365 for free using their student email.
options.B: Microsoft Office includes Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
options.C: I use free alternatives like Google Docs.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about software availability, so confirming access and explaining how to get it is directly helpful.

questions.stem: I can't figure out how to set up my student email on my phone. Can you help?

options.A: Sure, go to Settings and add the account using your full student email address.
options.B: Student emails are provided by the university.
options.C: I check my email on my laptop.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for technical help with email setup, so offering clear instructions is helpful.

questions.stem: The online submission portal keeps giving me an error message. I'm so frustrated.

options.A: Try clearing your browser cache and using Chrome. That worked for me.
options.B: Online portals are designed for convenience.

questions.explanation: The speaker is frustrated with a technical problem, so offering a specific practical solution is helpful.

questions.stem: Are the computer labs open 24 hours during finals week?

options.A: Yes, two labs in the library will be open 24/7 during exam period.
options.B: Computer labs have a variety of software installed.
options.C: I use my personal laptop for studying.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about access hours during a specific period, so providing the detailed schedule directly answers their question.

questions.stem: I just found out the library has free access to LinkedIn Learning courses!

options.A: That's great to know! I've been wanting to learn graphic design.
options.B: LinkedIn Learning has thousands of video tutorials.
options.C: I already have a LinkedIn Learning subscription.

questions.explanation: The speaker is sharing useful information, so showing enthusiasm and engaging with the opportunity is the natural response.

questions.stem: Should I keep my camera on during the online lecture or is it optional?

options.A: The professor prefers cameras on, but you can turn it off if your connection is unstable.
options.B: Online lectures are recorded and posted later.
options.C: I usually keep my camera off during class.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about expected behavior in an online class, so providing the professor's preference and exception is helpful.

questions.stem: I can help you set up the citation manager if you're still struggling with it.

options.A: That would be amazing! I've been stuck on this for hours.

questions.explanation: The speaker is offering assistance, so gratefully accepting the offer is the natural and polite response.

audioSegments.text: The campus Wi-Fi keeps disconnecting in my dorm. Is there a solution?
Does the school provide free access to Microsoft Office for students?
I can't figure out how to set up my student email on my phone. Can you help?
The online submission portal keeps giving me an error message. I'm so frustrated.
Are the computer labs open 24 hours during finals week?
